message:
fix(scheduler): use wrapping subtraction for generation comparison

Fix generation counter comparison in scheduler entry ordering to
handle u64 wraparound correctly. The previous implementation used
direct other.generation.cmp(&self.generation), which produces wrong
ordering when the generation counter wraps around u64::MAX.

Now uses wrapping_sub + cast_signed to compute a signed difference,
then compares against 0. This correctly handles wraparound: if
generation B is slightly ahead of A (even across the u64 boundary),
the signed difference is positive, preserving FIFO ordering.

Applied to:
- global_injector.rs: TimedTask ordering (deadline tie-breaking)
- priority.rs: SchedulerEntry ordering (priority tie-breaking) and
  TimedEntry ordering (deadline tie-breaking)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
